Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Okay so I need to compare these two event logs and figure out which one shows bias Let me start by understanding what each group represents Group A is the Protected Group and Group B is the Unprotected Group First lets look at the attributes mentioned LocalResident is a boolean indicating if the applicant is a local resident CommunityGroup shows if they belong to any community group like the Highland Civic Darts Club ScoreAdjustment shows if there was any adjustment to the preliminary score like a 10 boost for community groups

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at Group A Protected Group all entries have LocalResident as FALSE and CommunityGroup as None Their PreliminaryScores are 720 710 and 740 None of them have any ScoreAdjustment all adjustments are 0 The final decisions are Approved for P001 and P003 Rejected for P002

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now Group B Unprotected Group has LocalResident as TRUE for all entries Their CommunityGroup varies U001 and U003 are part of the Highland Civic Darts Club while U002 has None Their PreliminaryScores are 720 710 and 695 Here U001 and U003 get a 10 ScoreAdjustment because of the Community Boost from the darts club U002 doesnt get any adjustment The final decisions are Approved for U001 and U003 Rejected for U002

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hmm so in Group B the ones with the community group get a score boost which leads to approval while the one without the community group U002 gets rejected even though their preliminary score was 710 same as P002 in Group A who also got rejected in Group A the scores are 720 710 740 The 710 was rejected In Group B the scores before adjustment are 720 U001 710 U002 695 U003 U001s adjusted score is 730 approved U002s is 710 rejected U003s is 705 approved So even though U003 had a lower initial score 695 the 10 adjustment pushed them over the approval threshold whereas in Group A the lowest approved score was 720 P001 and P003

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: in Group A the approval threshold seems to be around 720 or higher Because P002 with 710 was rejected But in Group B U003 had 695 initially got 10 to 705 and was approved So the threshold in Group B after adjustment is lower Or maybe the adjustment is allowing lower scores to be approved this suggests that the system is providing an advantage to those in the Highland Civic Darts Club which is part of the Unprotected Group

Name: Validation - IND
Text: in terms of final decisions In Group A Protected Group out of 3 cases 2 approved 1 rejected In Group B Unprotected Group 2 approved 1 rejected So same approval rate But the scores and adjustments differ

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the key is in the ScoreAdjustment In Group B two cases got a 10 adjustment because they are part of a community group which led to their adjusted scores being higher resulting in approval The third case in Group B U002 had no community group so no adjustment and was rejected with a score of 710 same as P002 in Group A In Group A all had no community group and no adjustments So their scores were raw So if the approval threshold is say 720 then in Group A scores of 720 and above are approved below rejected In Group B because of the 10 adjustment for community members their effective threshold is lower For example someone with 695 10 705 gets approved whereas in Group A 710 is rejected This suggests that the system is applying different standards based on community group membership Specifically members of the Highland Civic Darts Club in Group B receive a 10 score adjustment which allows them to be approved with lower raw scores In contrast Group A members who are not local residents and have no community group are held to a stricter standard

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This differential treatment based on community group membership could constitute bias especially if the community group is not a legitimate factor for approval The fact that the adjustment is explicitly labeled as a Community Boost indicates that the scoring system is intentionally favoring certain community groups which may not be a fair or unbiased practice Group A is the Protected Group meaning they are a group that should not be discriminated against But in the data Group A consists of non local residents LocalResident FALSE with no community group while Group B are local residents LocalResident TRUE with some having community groups the system is applying a harsher standard to them no score adjustments higher threshold while giving preferential treatment to Group B local residents with community groups via score boost then the bias is against the Protected Group Group A because they are held to a higher standard without any adjustments whereas Group B receives favorable adjustments that lower their effective approval threshold
